Confidential Incident Report — AlBadr Materials PJSC (CR 1012345678) — Riyadh, KSA
Prepared by: Mr. Nasser Al‑Mutairi, Compliance Officer; contact: compliance@albadrmaterials.sa
Subject: Cyber incident affecting supplier portal and ESG data room linked to proposed green sukuk issuance (non‑public)
Summary: On 12/05/2026 02:40 AST, SOC detected unauthorized API calls from IP 185.82.14.77 to vendor.albadr.sa; intrusion window 17 minutes; system isolated within 6 minutes of alert
Data impact: limited exfiltration of two employee files containing national ID/Iqama numbers (e.g., Iqama 245‑88‑0193‑5 for Eng. Salim Al‑Qahtani and Iqama 203‑11‑7745‑2 for Ms. Lulwa Al‑Rasheed) and one supplier CR (CR 2059988776)
Regulatory actions: PDPL Article 17 notification submitted to SDAIA within 72 hours; CMA and Tadawul Market Surveillance informed due to potential MNPI exposure
MNPI context: ESG/climate financing workstream “Project Date Palm” relates to a sustainability‑linked sukuk with Najd Renewables PJSC (term sheet: coupon 3.45%, step‑up KPIs on Scope 2 reduction) — not publicly announced
Information barriers: insider list created (v3) for Project Date Palm; trading blackout window enforced for listed securities of AlBadr Materials PJSC and Najd Renewables PJSC; access restricted to need‑to‑know
Crypto/DPT aspect: the ESG data room contains a sandbox pilot for tokenized carbon credits on “HejazChain” with test wallet addresses; no tokens have been offered to the public; disclosure sequencing under review
Commercial terms at risk: draft EPC addendum with Al‑Hasa Energy Co. (liquidated damages at SAR 12,000/day cap, FX collar 3.74–3.79) was stored in the affected folder; counterparties have not been notified pending counsel
Containment: credentials rotated; NCA guidance followed; forensic images secured; no evidence of lateral movement to ERP or payroll; customer PII unaffected
External dialogue: no selective disclosure; all counterpart queries routed to IR (ir@albadrmaterials.sa); any approach by unknown parties to be documented and escalated to CMA Market Conduct
Cross‑border transfer: temporary log analysis by a processor in Manama requires SDAIA approval; data minimization applied and identifiers masked before export
Training note (benign): our marketing team will host a public webinar on crypto wallets, insider lists, and blackout windows as general education for SMEs; it will not discuss any issuer event or investment terms
Next steps: board sign‑off on disclosure timing; coordinate with legal adviser at Diriyah Legal Offices; prepare Tadawul announcement template in case materiality threshold is met
Contact: Mr. Nasser Al‑Mutairi (+966‑11‑555‑0199), compliance@albadrmaterials.sa; escalation alternate Ms. Reem Al‑Otaibi, DPO, dpo@albadrmaterials.sa
